_meta.descripcion: Extensión del banco diagnóstico al nivel Pro (P1 + P2, solo semanas con gramática nueva). Mismo esquema que Basic/Advanced. Se añade a BANK.topics y se extiende PLACE_MAP.
_meta.integracion: Añade estos 6 temas al final de BANK.topics (después de present_perfect_continuous). Orden = gradiente: Basic 0-7, Advanced 8-15, Pro 16-21. Extiende PLACE_MAP.
_meta.nota_semanas_tematicas: P2 Sem3 (Job Interview), P2 Sem4 (Psychology) y todo P Mix NO llevan gramática nueva — son aplicación, no generan bloque de diagnóstico. Su vocab vive en periplo_vocab_pro.csv.
_meta.nota_vocab: No se proporcionó archivo de Noun/Adj Packs 'Pro'. Usé vocab temático natural por lectura (Law & Politics, Finance, Science, etc.). Si luego defines listas Pro estrictas, se ajusta.
_meta.nota_ladder: Pro es la cima de la escalera adaptativa. Trueno de banda = fin del examen; placement = banda más alta superada.

_legend.skill: punto de fallo que mide (tag para el rubro)
_legend.L1: true=caza error típico de transferencia del español
_legend.contrast: true=opciones malas de estructuras que compiten

skills_propuestas: Contraste PP vs Past Perfect (orden temporal)

questions.q: The government ___ passed three new laws this year.
By the time the scandal broke, the minister ___ already resigned.

questions.audioText: They have reformed the policy, but the damage had already been done.
questions.q: Which happened first?
Match each verb with its past participle.
Parliament ___ (vote) on the bill three times this month.
Before the election, the party ___ (promise) a major reform.

questions.skill: Contraste PP vs Past Perfect (orden temporal)
questions.answer: She had already left when I arrived.

options: Yes, it has. They voted last week.
questions.answer: Yes, it has. They voted last week.

questions.q: Select ALL the grammatically correct sentences.
options: They had already voted before noon.
answers: They had already voted before noon.

questions.q: The president has went to the summit.
questions.answer: The president has gone to the summit.

questions.skill: Contraste PP vs Past Perfect (orden temporal)
questions.q: I have seen the minister yesterday.
When the police arrived, the suspect already escaped.
questions.answer: When the police arrived, the suspect had already escaped.

skills_propuestas: If + had + participio, would have + participio

questions.skill: If + had + participio, would have + participio
questions.q: If the bank had regulated itself, the crash ___.

questions.skill: If + had + participio, would have + participio
questions.q: The third conditional talks about imaginary situations in the PAST.

questions.audioText: If I had invested earlier, I would be retired now.
questions.q: Did the person invest early?
Match each 'if' clause with its result.

pairs.left: If I hadn't taken the loan,
pairs.right: I wouldn't be in debt now.

questions.skill: If + had + participio, would have + participio
questions.q: If he ___ (invest) in stocks, he would have made a profit.
If they had cut costs, they ___ (avoid) bankruptcy.

questions.answer: If I had studied finance, I would be richer now.

options: If I had saved, I wouldn't be broke now.
If I would save, I wouldn't be broke.
If I saved, I won't be broke.
If I have saved, I am not broke.
questions.answer: If I had saved, I wouldn't be broke now.

questions.q: Select ALL the grammatically correct sentences.
options: If I had known, I would have sold.
If I would have known, I would have sold.
If she had invested, she'd be rich now.
If she would have invested, she'd be rich.
answers: If I had known, I would have sold.
If she had invested, she'd be rich now.

questions.q: If I would have had money, I would have invested.
questions.answer: If I had had money, I would have invested.

questions.q: I would of bought the stock earlier.
questions.answer: I would have bought the stock earlier.

questions.q: If they had not defaulted, they would not lost everything.
questions.answer: If they had not defaulted, they would not have lost everything.

Sort each sentence into ACTIVE or PASSIVE.

questions.skill: Tiempos de la pasiva (was/is/has been + participio)
questions.q: The data ___ (collect) over five years.
The conclusion was ___ from the data. (draw)

questions.answer: The discovery was made by accident.

It was wrote by Dr. Lee.

questions.skill: Tiempos de la pasiva (was/is/has been + participio)

options: The medicine was tested on animals.
The medicine was test on animals.
answers: The medicine was tested on animals.

The experiment did by a team of scientists.

questions.q: I ___ studied harder for that exam.

He regrets it now. He ___ (act) sooner.

context: A: Why didn't you warn her?

I should had, but I didn't.
I should of, but I didn't.
I should have to, but I didn't.

questions.answer: You should have asked for help.

questions.q: She could have went, but she stayed.
questions.answer: She could have gone, but she stayed.

questions.q: I would have call you, but I lost your number.
questions.answer: I would have called you, but I lost your number.

questions.q: She ___ that the project was delayed.

He said me he was leaving.
She asked what time it was.
She asked what time was it.
answers: He told me he was leaving.
She asked what time it was.

questions.answer: I decided to quit my job.

context: A: Have you thought about it?

I enjoy to read every night.

questions.answer: I look forward to meeting you.

discrimination_set._nota: Discriminación de estructuras Pro (PP vs Past Perfect vs Past Simple, conditionals, activa/pasiva, gerundio/infinitivo, reported speech). Checkpoint al entrar a Pro.

questions.q: Choose the correct sentence (with 'yesterday').
Choose the PAST imaginary sentence.
options: If I had money, I would buy it.
If I had had money, I would have bought it.
If I have money, I buy it.
If I would have money, I buy it.
questions.answer: If I had had money, I would have bought it.

questions.q: Choose the correct passive sentence.
Choose the correct sentence.
He asked me where did I live.
questions.answer: He asked me where I lived.

questions.q: Select ALL the grammatically correct sentences.
Choose the correct sentence for an action still in progress.
options: I have been working here for years.
I have worked here since I work.
I am work here for years.
questions.answer: I have been working here for years.

questions.q: She has wrote three reports this week.
questions.answer: She has written three reports this week.

exam2_productive._nota: Producción para el Examen 2 (auto:false, lo revisa el maestro). Temas de Pro.

questions.prompt: Describe a famous discovery using the passive voice (3 sentences). E.g. 'It was discovered that...'.
If you could change one decision in your life, what would have happened differently? Speak for 3 sentences.

questions.q: Ella me dijo que había terminado el proyecto.
questions.answer: She told me that she had finished the project.

questions.prompt: What are the benefits of doing hard things? Write 3 sentences using gerunds.

questions.q: Si hubiera estudiado más, habría aprobado el examen.
questions.answer: If I had studied more, I would have passed the exam.

questions.prompt: Talk about something you have achieved, and what you had already done before that. Use present perfect and past perfect.
